project_description: Marketing front for Elitez LMS — a Singapore-built LMS that turns a client's existing training materials (sales pitch decks, training decks, employee handbooks, brand books, product catalogues) into modular training units + quizzes. Out-of-the-box library of 1,200+ ready-made SG-relevant questions (HR / cyber / AI / productivity) is included from the entry tier. Pricing starts at zero and tops out at S$299 + S$2.99 per employee per month.
constraints: B2B institutional tone — Elitez Group brand register (BCA / PDPC / IHRP-adjacent); no marketing-speak, no emojis in product copy.
Footer attribution: 'Operated by Elitez Group of Companies' linking to https://www.elitez.asia.
Logo file canonical at /public/elitez-logo.png; small / corner placement only.
Eventual production domain: TBD (the app lives at lms.elitez.com.sg; marketing site needs a sub-domain or sub-path decision before announcement).

Do NOT lift dormitory / blue-collar / frontline / outsourced-worker framing from anywhere — this LMS trains office staff (Recruitment Consultants, BD Executives, Account Managers, HR Operations, but ALSO mid-market SME teams of any white-collar role once we open the door to BYO content). Anti-audience signals are explicit below.

DO NOT pitch on Singapore government training grants — no PSG / WDG(JR+) / SCTP / SkillsFuture Mid-Career / SFEC / IBF-STS / ETSS framing on the marketing site. The grants exist and we will mention them in private sales conversations on request, but the marketing pitch must stand on its own product value: BYO content, AI-generated quizzes, low entry price.

narrative_brief.what_the_product_does: A Singapore-built LMS with two entry doors. Door one: a ready-made library of 56 modules and 1,200+ quiz questions covering SG HR compliance (Employment Act Part IV, CPF, work passes EP/SP/WP, TAFEP / Fair Consideration, KETs, salary / leave / termination / retrenchment, IR8A / IR21, WSH, TADM, Re-employment Act); cyber hygiene (phishing, password / MFA, device / BYOD, incident reporting); AI literacy (LLM concepts, prompting, AI risks, AI-at-work policy); productivity (Google Workspace, Excel, PowerPoint, PDFs, Sign Now, meeting hygiene); recruitment + BD craft (Boolean search, LinkedIn sourcing, candidate interviews, BANT / MEDDIC, outreach, proposals, account management). Door two: upload your own sales decks, training decks, employee handbooks, brand books, product catalogues — the LMS reverse-engineers them into modular training units, with quizzes generated against the content. Role-keyed onboarding plans, manager dashboards with M1 / M2 / M3 progress, org-wide admin view, audience-scoped module visibility, CSV / API exports on higher tiers.
narrative_brief.primary_audience: Singapore SMEs and mid-market teams (30-500 headcount) with existing training material that lives in PowerPoint, PDF, and shared drives — sales decks, onboarding handbooks, product catalogues, brand books. The buyer is an Ops Director or HR Manager who has tried to roll out training before and lost three weeks turning decks into something trainees can actually click through. EA-licensed recruitment / staffing agencies (~1,200 SG entities) remain the highest-affinity wedge because of the ready-made compliance content overlap. HRMS / payroll / EOR providers (Talenox, JustLogin, Deel SG, Multiplier SG) for the white-label channel. SG SME professional-services firms (consultancies, advisories, law firms, design studios) for the BYO-deck wedge.
value_props: Reverse-engineer your decks — drop in PPT, PDF, DOCX; we turn them into modular learning units within hours.
AI-generated quizzes from your uploaded content — every module ships with assessment questions on first publish.
1,200+ ready-made SG-relevant questions out of the box — cover the standard SG HR / cyber / AI / productivity baseline from day one.
Branded delivery — your logo, your tone, your modules; trainees never see Elitez's brand if you choose white-label.
Manager dashboards with M1 / M2 / M3 RAG progress, audience scoping, CSV / API exports.
Singapore-built, PDPA-aligned residency — content stays on SG-region infrastructure.
differentiators_vs_competitors: Articulate Rise / iSpring Learn / Tovuti require manual authoring per module — they don't reverse-engineer existing decks; they expect you to rebuild slide-by-slide.
Coursebox AI / EduFlow / Tovuti AI-author features generate generic content from prompts; we ingest YOUR content (your decks, your tone, your products) and structure it.
Cornerstone / SAP SuccessFactors Learning / Workday Learning cost 5-15x our price and require enterprise procurement; we start at zero.
EdApp / Disprz target frontline workforces; we target white-collar office teams who actually have decks to upload.
DIY SharePoint + Forms + WhatsApp is the actual incumbent we displace — cheap and sticky, but the manager has no analytics and no audit trail.

tier_ladder.name: Starter
tier_ladder.headline: Up to 5 seats
what_in: Read-only access to the Elitez library

tier_ladder.name: Team
tier_ladder.headline: Full Elitez library + light branding
what_in: Your logo + company name on existing decks

tier_ladder.name: Pro
tier_ladder.headline: Upload your decks → reverse-engineered modules
what_in: Upload 5-8 of your own decks; reverse-engineered into modules
Quizzes auto-generated against your uploaded content

tier_ladder.name: Scale
tier_ladder.headline: AI quiz generation + role tracks + branded delivery
what_in: Up to 20 custom modules from uploads
AI-generated quiz items from your uploaded content (not just hand-mapped)
Role-based learning tracks (build paths per job family)

tier_ladder.name: Enterprise
tier_ladder.headline: SSO, white-label, integrations, dedicated CSM
what_in: White-label + custom domain (clients never see Elitez's brand)

pricing_principles: Free + min-8-seats-paid is intentionally low-friction — the buyer should be able to swipe a card without procurement involvement up to 50-100 seats.
Per-seat $2.99 is constant across all paid tiers — the platform-flat fee is what scales for capability, not the per-head charge.
No SG-government-grant framing on the marketing site. Grants get discussed privately on demand if a buyer asks.
No annual lock-in language. Month-to-month positioning; annual commitments can be negotiated on the Scale + Enterprise tiers in private.
audience_signals: SG SMEs and mid-market teams have decks they wrote but never operationalised — onboarding decks, sales pitch decks, brand books, product catalogues, SOP manuals.
Managers consistently report that they spend 5-15 hours per quarter answering the same training questions one-on-one because the deck is in a shared drive nobody reads.
Post-2026 GenAI rollouts have created a new training surface (AI use policy, prompt craft, AI risks) that none of the major LMS vendors ship out of the box.
PDPA + IMDA AI Verify guidance pushes SG SMEs toward localized compliance content vs adapting US/EU/IN parent LMS content.
Sub-100-staff SG firms either DIY (SharePoint / Notion / WhatsApp) or buy a generic global LMS — neither path turns their existing decks into a structured training programme.
anti_audience_signals: Not for dormitory operators or blue-collar frontline workers — that was a previous run's framing error and is permanently fenced off.
Not for >5,000-seat enterprises where Cornerstone / Workday / SAP procurement processes are already in flight.
Not for SMS-OTP / no-email / multilingual-mobile use cases — we are browser-based, English-first, email-OTP.
